questions.prompt_html:
Provide an appropriate response.

Consider the graphs of y = cos-1 x and y = sin-1 x. Does it make sense that the derivatives of these functions are opposites? Explain.

questions.prompt_html: Prove that if an odd function f(x) is one-to-one, the inverse function g(y) is also odd.
questions.prompt_text: Prove that if an odd function f(x) is one-to-one, the inverse function g(y) is also odd.
questions.solution_html:
If y = f-1(-x), then - x = f(y), so x = -f(y) = f(-y) since f is odd.
Thus - y = f-1(x), and so f-1(-x) = y = -f-1(x). Thus, f-1 is odd.
questions.solution_text: If y = f -1 (-x), then - x = f(y), so x = -f(y) = f(-y) since f is odd. Thus - y = f -1 (x), and so f -1 (-x) = y = -f -1 (x). Thus, f -1 is odd.

feedback_html.solution:
If y = f-1(-x), then - x = f(y), so x = -f(y) = f(-y) since f is odd.
Thus - y = f-1(x), and so f-1(-x) = y = -f-1(x). Thus, f-1 is odd.

questions.prompt_html: Sketch the graph of f(x) = tan<sup>-1</sup>(tan x), showing at least the part between x = -2π and x = 2π. What is the domain of f?
questions.prompt_text: Sketch the graph of f(x) = tan -1 (tan x), showing at least the part between x = -2π and x = 2π. What is the domain of f?
questions.solution_html: The domain of f(x) = tan-1(tan x) consists of all real numbers except the numbers  + kπ, where k is any integer.
questions.solution_text: Short Description: A graph of the inverse of a tangent function represents parallel slanted lines.Long Description: The x axis ranges from negative 2.25 pi to 2.25 pi in increments of pi over 4, and the y axis ranges from negative pi to pi in increments of pi over 4. The lines slopes upward passing through the points (negative 2 pi, 0), (negative pi, 0), (0, 0), (pi, 0), and (2 pi, 0). The domain of f(x) = tan -1 (tan x) consists of all real numbers except the numbers (π/2) + kπ, where k is any integer.
